notes: Reproduced on SkiaSharp 2.88.3: DllNotFoundException for libSkiaSharp occurs at runtime in browser because the .a files are not statically linked into dotnet.wasm. The 2.88.x NativeAssets.WebAssembly package did not auto-configure NativeFileReference for Blazor WASM apps. Fixed in SkiaSharp 3.x: both SkiaSharp.Views.Blazor 3.119.2 and plain SkiaSharp + NativeAssets.WebAssembly 3.119.2 work correctly — the .targets file now auto-includes NativeFileReference when building for WASM. The comment #3 claim of 3.119.0 failure could not be reproduced with 3.119.2; likely a configuration issue on the reporter's end.

reproductionSteps.description: Create Blazor WebAssembly project with SkiaSharp 2.88.3 + NativeAssets.WebAssembly (reporter's exact setup, no WasmBuildNative)
reproductionSteps.command: dotnet new blazorwasm -n Repro --framework net10.0 && cd Repro && dotnet add package SkiaSharp --version 2.88.3 && dotnet add package SkiaSharp.NativeAssets.WebAssembly --version 2.88.3

filesCreated.description: Blazor page using SKImageInfo and SKSurface.Create to reproduce the DllNotFoundException

reproductionSteps.description: Build Blazor WASM project with SkiaSharp 2.88.3
Run Blazor WASM app and check browser console — DllNotFoundException: libSkiaSharp at runtime
reproductionSteps.output:
Browser console errors:
[ERROR] System.DllNotFoundException: libSkiaSharp
[ERROR]    at SkiaSharp.SKImageInfo..cctor()
[LOG] ERROR: TypeInitializationException: TypeInitialization_Type, SkiaSharp.SKImageInfo
[LOG] INNER: DllNotFoundException: libSkiaSharp

Page shows: Status: ERROR: TypeInitializationException: TypeInitialization_Type, SkiaSharp.SKImageInfo

reproductionSteps.description: Test SkiaSharp 3.119.2 with SkiaSharp.Views.Blazor in fresh project — works correctly
reproductionSteps.command: dotnet new blazorwasm -n Repro --framework net10.0 && cd Repro && dotnet add package SkiaSharp.Views.Blazor --version 3.119.2 && dotnet build && dotnet run --urls http://localhost:5112 --no-build
reproductionSteps.output: Build succeeded with native linking (libSkiaSharp.a linked into dotnet.native.wasm). Browser console: SUCCESS: rendered 1024x1024. No errors.

reproductionSteps.description: Test SkiaSharp 3.119.2 with plain SkiaSharp + NativeAssets.WebAssembly in fresh project — works correctly
reproductionSteps.command: dotnet new blazorwasm -n Repro --framework net10.0 && cd Repro && dotnet add package SkiaSharp --version 3.119.2 && dotnet add package SkiaSharp.NativeAssets.WebAssembly --version 3.119.2 && dotnet build && dotnet run --urls http://localhost:5113 --no-build
reproductionSteps.output: Build succeeded with native linking (libSkiaSharp.a auto-linked). Browser console: SUCCESS: SKImageInfo and SKSurface created successfully. No errors.

versionResults.notes: DllNotFoundException: libSkiaSharp at runtime in browser. The 2.88.x NativeAssets.WebAssembly package does not auto-configure NativeFileReference for Blazor WASM apps.
Both SkiaSharp.Views.Blazor and plain SkiaSharp + NativeAssets.WebAssembly work correctly. The .targets file auto-includes NativeFileReference for WASM builds.

corrections.corrected: Confirmed fixed in 3.119.2. The fix from PR #3082 works correctly. Comment #3 claim of 3.119.0 failure could not be reproduced with 3.119.2.

actionability.reason: The DllNotFoundException is reproduced on 2.88.3 but fully fixed in 3.119.2 with both SkiaSharp.Views.Blazor and plain NativeAssets.WebAssembly. The fix from PR #3082 correctly auto-configures NativeFileReference for WASM builds.

actions.description: Add area/wasm label to clarify this is a WASM-specific packaging issue
Close as fixed — the issue is resolved in SkiaSharp 3.x. Suggest upgrade.

proposedResponse.body:
Thanks for reporting this! We were able to reproduce the `TypeInitializationException` with inner `DllNotFoundException: libSkiaSharp` using SkiaSharp 2.88.3 in a Blazor WebAssembly app.

The root cause is that in SkiaSharp 2.x, the `NativeAssets.WebAssembly` package did not automatically configure the `NativeFileReference` needed to statically link `libSkiaSharp.a` into `dotnet.native.wasm` at build time.

The good news is that this is **fixed in SkiaSharp 3.x**. We tested with SkiaSharp 3.119.2 and confirmed it works correctly with both:
- `SkiaSharp.Views.Blazor` (recommended for Blazor apps)
- Plain `SkiaSharp` + `SkiaSharp.NativeAssets.WebAssembly`

**Tested versions:**
| Version | Result |
|---------|--------|
| 2.88.3 | ❌ DllNotFoundException: libSkiaSharp |
| 3.119.2 | ✅ Fixed — renders correctly |

**To fix this, upgrade to SkiaSharp 3.x:**
```xml
<PackageReference Include="SkiaSharp.Views.Blazor" Version="3.119.2" />
```

If you must stay on 2.x, you can manually add the native file reference as described in [this comment](https://github.com/mono/SkiaSharp/issues/2732#issuecomment-2501856316).

@tolzy88 — Regarding your report of the same issue on 3.119.0 with .NET 8: we were unable to reproduce this with 3.119.2. Could you share your `.csproj` file and confirm which packages you're referencing? The `NativeAssets.WebAssembly` package should auto-configure everything in 3.x.

workarounds: Upgrade to SkiaSharp 3.x (3.119.2 or later) which auto-configures WASM native linking
For 2.x: Manually add <NativeFileReference Include="$(SkiaSharpStaticLibraryPath)\3.1.34\st\*.a" /> to the project file
missingInfo: Comment #3 reporter (tolzy88): .csproj file contents showing package references and any custom MSBuild properties
Comment #3 reporter: exact .NET SDK version (dotnet --info output)
